seoTitle: Mount Peter vs Mount Pleasant — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Peter and Mount Pleasant. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Peter vs Mount Pleasant: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Peter and Mount Pleasant.
heading: Mount Peter vs Mount Pleasant — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Peter, Queensland and Mount Pleasant, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Peter has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Mount Pleasant (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Peter or Mount Pleasant safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Peter has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Mount Pleasant scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Peter scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Pleasant scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Peter or Mount Pleasant?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Peter has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
